LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF SPIRE GLOBAL SUBSIDIARY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. PLAINTIFF SPIRE GLOBAL SUBSIDIARY, INC. ("SPIRE") MOVES FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON ITS SINGLE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AGAINST DEFENDANT ASTRA SPACE OPERATIONS, LLC ("ASTRA"). SPIRE'S MOTION IS GRANTED. THE COURT MAKES NO DETERMINATION ABOUT ATTORNEYS' FEES IN THIS MOTION. (SEE CAL. RULES OF COURT, RULE 3.1702.)THE COURT CONCLUDES SPIRE IS ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT ON ITS BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION IN THE AMOUNT OF $400,000. ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar May-29-2026 Discharged

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar May-11-2026 Continued To Jul-08-26 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 505

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Prejudgment Interest; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Jeffrey S. Goodfried In Support
Off Calendar Feb-11-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF SPIRE GLOBAL SUBSIDIARY, INC.'s Motion For Prejudgment Interest.

The unopposed motion for prejudgment interest is granted for good cause shown in the moving papers. Moving party shall submit a proposed amended judgment, which the court will hold for 10 days for objections.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Courts ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-15-2026 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-11-2025 Continued To Feb-02-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-18-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF SPIRE GLOBAL SUBSIDIARY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Plaintiff Spire Global Subsidiary, Inc. ("Spire") moves for summary judgment on its single cause of action for breach of contract against defendant Astra Space Operations, LLC ("Astra"). Spire's motion is granted.

The court makes no determination about attorneys' fees in this motion. (See Cal. Rules of Court, Rule 3.1702.)

The court concludes Spire is entitled to judgment on its breach of contract cause of action in the amount of $400,000.

(see word minutes for complete ruling)

Order is signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
